key_findings: HbA1c is the strongest predictor of retinopathy risk
Diabetes duration has compounding effect, especially with poor control
Regular retinal screening is protective against advanced retinopathy
Model aligns with established clinical guidelines
clinical_recommendations: Focus on HbA1c control (<7.0% target for most patients)
Ensure annual retinal screening for all diabetic patients
Aggressive blood pressure management in high-risk patients
Prioritize patients with >10 years diabetes duration for screening
Consider more frequent screening for patients with multiple risk factors
